column1: "text": "A computer system consists of two fundamental parts: Hardware and Software. Understanding how these components interact is essential for anyone learning to program."
"text": "Every computer system has five main hardware components that work together to process information:"
"text": ": the brain of the computer that executes instructions"
"text": ": memory locations containing the currently running program"

column3: is a long list of memory locations where the computer can directly access any location at any time. Think of it like a massive wall of mailboxes
column4: each with its own unique address."

column1: "text": "A digit that can only be zero or one"
"text": "A memory location consisting of eight bits"
"text": "A unique number that identifies each memory location"
"text": "Each memory location contains a pattern of zeros and ones. These patterns can change during program execution as the computer reads and writes data."
"text": "Here is a fascinating puzzle: the binary pattern 01000001 could mean three completely different things depending on context:"
"text": " if interpreted as code to execute"

"text": "So how does the computer know what 01000001 really means? The interpretation depends entirely on the current instruction being executed. The CPU tracks what type of data it expects to find at each memory address. As a programmer
column2: you rarely need to worry about this \u2014 the programming language and operating system handle it for you."

column1: "text": "Some types of data are simply too large to fit into a single byte. For example
column2: most integers and real numbers require multiple bytes of storage."

the address refers to the first byte in the sequence. The next few consecutive bytes store the additional bits needed for the complete value. The computer knows how many bytes to read based on the data type."

"text": "While main memory (RAM) stores instructions and data while a program is actively running
column2: secondary memory serves a different purpose: permanent storage."

column1: "text": ": Temporary storage \u2014 contents are lost when power is turned off"
"text": ": Permanent storage \u2014 retains instructions and data even after switching the computer off and on again"
"text": "A file is simply a collection of data or instructions stored in secondary memory. When you save your work
column2: you are writing a file to secondary memory."

column1: "text": "The Central Processing Unit (CPU) is typically called the processor. It follows program instructions and is capable of performing fundamental operations including:"
"text": " \u2014 transfer information from one memory location to another"
"text": "A computer program is a set of instructions for a computer to follow. Computer software is the collection of programs used by a computer. It includes:"
"text": " \u2014 tools for writing and modifying text and code"
"text": " \u2014 programs that convert code into machine language"
"text": " \u2014 core services that manage the computer's operations"
"text": "Allows us to communicate with the computer"
"text": "Built to run on top of the operating system"
"text": "Responds to user requests to run other programs"
"text": "The operating system is itself a program \u2014 a very important one that manages everything else."
"text": "Input to a computer system consists of two things:"
"text": " \u2014 the set of instructions to be executed"
"text": " \u2014 the information the program will process"

column2: the program and its data form the complete input that a computer needs to perform useful work."

column1: "text": "A software system consists of sets of programs. To create programs effectively
column2: you need to understand two key concepts: algorithms and programs."

column1: "text": "A sequence of precise instructions that leads to a solution"
"text": "An algorithm expressed in a language the computer can understand"
"text": "Written in a human-readable language (like pseudocode or plain English)"
"text": "Think of an algorithm as a recipe written for a human
column2: and a program as that same recipe translated into a language a cooking robot would understand."

"text": ": Spread peanut butter on one slice"
"text": ": Place the second slice on top"
"text": "Notice how each step is precise and unambiguous. There is no room for confusion \u2014 exactly what a computer needs."
"text": "Programming is a creative process \u2014 there are many different ways to create a program that solves the same problem. The program design process is divided into two main phases:"
"text": ": The result is an algorithm that solves the problem"
"text": ": The result is the algorithm translated into a programming language"

"text": "Develop the algorithm before implementation. Experience shows this saves significant time in getting your program to run correctly. Always test the algorithm for correctness before coding."
"text": "Once your algorithm is ready and tested
